$schema-note: Canonical machine-readable encoding of SPEC.md §3 (required keys per type), §4 (required L2 headings per type), and §12 (per-section body schema). The prose at /research/flexible-frontmatter-toolchain/output/SPEC.md is the human-readable source; this JSON is the binding source for tooling. fm-validate MUST load all matrices from this file; hardcoding the matrices in Python is forbidden.
task_status_doc: Lifecycle states for the L2 task_status field. 'updated' is closure-with-continuity (TASK.md §4.7): the Task is closed but a successor referenced via task_supersedes/task_superseded_by carries the intent forward.

key_docs.task_blocked_by: List of task_id (or slug) values whose task_status MUST be 'done' before this Task may transition open → in_progress (TASK.md §8.7).
key_docs.task_supersedes: List of predecessor task_id (or slug) values that this Task replaces. Reciprocal with task_superseded_by. Set on a successor created via the 'updated' lifecycle (TASK.md §4.7).
key_docs.task_superseded_by: List of successor task_id (or slug) values. Set on the predecessor at the moment task_status flips to 'updated'. Reciprocal with task_supersedes.

Goal.doc: One-paragraph statement of what success looks like.

Todo.doc: Checkbox state mirrors task_status: all checked → done.

Links.doc: Bulleted list of repo-relative markdown links.

prompt._required_headings_note: Empty for v1 (Task 017): existing prompts span multiple authoring eras (RISEN, RISEN+ReAct, custom). The body_schema below still encodes the RISEN+ReAct contract and is enforced under fm-validate --check-body; Task 019 flips --check-body to default-on once the corpus is migrated. SPEC §4.1 expects [Framework, R — Role, I — Input, S — Steps, E — Expectations, Constraints] — restore this list when the corpus conforms.

Constraints.doc: Each constraint SHOULD use one RFC 2119 keyword (lang-spec U1).

body_schema._note: Anthropic SKILL.md owns the body — no schema enforced.

reciprocity.note: Reciprocal slug-graph edges checked by fm-validate --type-check (F.T.2). Each rule names a forward field on the source type and the back-reference field expected on the target type. fm-validate walks every operational file, builds the slug→file index, and verifies every forward reference has a matching back-reference. Note: `prompt_spawned_from_research` is intentionally NOT listed here — the spawn relationship is one-to-many and the back-edge `research_executes_prompt` is a scalar that names only the original prompt, not the follow-ups.

path_classification.note: Maps file paths to expected type. The validator infers expected_type from path; the file's frontmatter type: MUST agree (or be one of the path-allowed alternates). Patterns are matched against repo-relative paths.
